description:
Goal: stop Compiler2 semantic analysis from exploding on the Enum.reduce backend-interpreter path.

Signal: `compiler2_interp_runs_enum_reduce_from_backend_artifacts` finishes quickly and returns 15, and the broader `compiler2_interp_` backend-interpreter slice no longer hangs.

Change:
- trace the recursive activation creation path triggered by Enum.reduce protocol/callable dispatch
- make `prepare_function_call -> activation_key -> widen_for_recursive_spec_key` converge without runaway cloning/intern churn
- keep the backend artifact/runtime seam unchanged; fix the semantic/type-key source of the blow-up

Why:
- validating `fz-rh2.8.2.5` showed the runtime intrinsic path is working, but Enum.reduce still hangs inside Compiler2 semantic analysis before backend execution
- a live sample pinned the hot path in `analyze_activation`, `prepare_function_call`, and `widen_for_recursive_spec_key`

Checks:
- `cargo test --lib compiler2_interp_runs_enum_reduce_from_backend_artifacts -- --nocapture` passes
- `cargo test --lib compiler2_interp_ -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` completes
- sampling no longer shows unbounded time in `widen_for_recursive_spec_key`
title: Compiler2 semantics: make recursive activation-key widening converge for Enum.reduce
